title: paracarphalea angulata
intro: Paracarphalea angulata, commonly known as the 'Angulate Paracarphalea', is a plant species from the family Rubiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of various diseases. The therapeutic actions of Paracarphalea angulata include its ability to act as an antimicrobial and anti-diabetic agent, and it may also exhibit anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents responsible for its therapeutic actions have not been extensively documented, but it is believed to contain alkaloids, flavonoids, and other secondary metabolites.
side_effects: Paracarphalea angulata can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to its extracts has also been linked to skin irritation, including redness, itching, and rashes. Furthermore, combining Paracarphalea angulata with certain medications, such as blood thinners, may exacerbate bleeding disorders.
